title: TCP/IP Application Layer
description: Combines OSI layers 5, 6, and 7 — handles all application protocols, encryption, and session management.
fullExplanation: The TCP/IP Application layer is the topmost layer and combines what OSI splits into three separate layers: Session (L5), Presentation (L6), and Application (L7). In practice, most protocols handle their own session management and data formatting internally. This layer is where all user-facing protocols live — HTTP/HTTPS for web, DNS for name resolution, SMTP/IMAP for email, SSH for remote access, DHCP for IP configuration, SNMP for network management, and many more.
keyFacts: Combines OSI Session + Presentation + Application into one layer
Each protocol handles its own session management internally
TLS provides the Presentation layer encryption function
DNS is the foundation — almost every other protocol depends on it
Application layer PDU is called 'Data' or 'Message'
Protocols communicate with Transport layer via sockets (port numbers)
